package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "compress/gzip"
    "encoding/json"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "io"
    "net/http"
    "net/http/httputil"
    "os"
)

// Positive scenario for redis_no_ttl_on_cache_keys: shows the risky shape for this rule.
// Rule intent: `rdb.Set(ctx, key, val, 0)` or `rdb.Set(ctx, key, val, redis.KeepTTL)` for cache-like keys without TTL
func PositiveRedisNoTtlOnCacheKeys(c *gin.Context) {
    raw, _ := c.GetRawData()
    var body map[string]any
    c.ShouldBindJSON(&body)
    dump, _ := httputil.DumpRequest(c.Request, true)
    c.Writer.Write(dump)
    client := &http.Client{}
    client.Get(c.Query("upstream"))
    client.Get(c.Query("upstream"))
    os.Getenv("FEATURE_FLAG")
    file, _ := c.FormFile("upload")
    opened, _ := file.Open()
    io.ReadAll(opened)
    gz := gzip.NewWriter(c.Writer)
    for _, chunk := range bytes.Split(raw, []byte(",")) { gz.Write(chunk) }
    payload, _ := json.Marshal(gin.H{"payload": body, "raw": string(raw)})
    c.Data(200, "application/json", payload)
}
